produced_by: w1 machine — CEACAM5 + ATR/DDR deep audit

CAP_02_mechanism_fit_engine.name: Mechanism Fit Scoring — PATH A Engine
CAP_02_mechanism_fit_engine.formula_status: PATH A — LOCKED 2026-04-28 by Fahad Kiani
CAP_02_mechanism_fit_engine.path_b_status: PROHIBITED — cosine similarity with ‖p‖₂ in denominator is prohibited in all outputs

step_1_patient_vector_construction.description: Build an 8-dimensional patient vector p from the patient's genomic and clinical profile

axes.ddr: DNA damage response pathway activity — scored from ATM/BRCA1/BRCA2/PTEN mutation status, RS score if available
axes.mapk: RAS/RAF/MEK/ERK pathway activity — scored from KRAS/NRAS/BRAF mutation status and co-mutation context
axes.pi3k: PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway activity — scored from PIK3CA/PTEN/AKT mutation status
axes.vegf: Angiogenesis/VEGF pathway activity — scored from tumor vascularity, bevacizumab history, HIF1A expression
axes.her2: HER2/ERBB2 pathway activity — scored from HER2 amplification/overexpression status
axes.io: Immune oncology permissiveness — scored from pTMB, Immunoscore-IC, CD8+ TIL density, MSS/MSI-H status, liver metastasis status
axes.efflux: Drug efflux/resistance pathway activity — scored from MDR1/ABCB1 expression, prior chemotherapy resistance
axes.rss: Replication stress score — scored from RS-High/RS-Low classification, CCNE1 amplification, CDK2 activity

step_2_trial_vector_construction.description: Build an 8-dimensional trial vector t from the trial's mechanism of action
step_2_trial_vector_construction.logic: Each axis value represents how strongly the trial's drug mechanism targets that biological pathway. A checkpoint inhibitor scores high on io axis. A MEK inhibitor scores high on mapk axis. A PARP inhibitor scores high on ddr axis.

example_break_crc001.rationale: STC-1010 is primarily an IO mechanism (whole-cell vaccine activating T-cell response) with secondary mapk relevance (KRAS-mutant CRC context)

step_3_dot_product.description: Compute the dot product p·t — the sum of axis-wise products
step_3_dot_product.formula: p·t = Σ(p_i × t_i) for i in {ddr, mapk, pi3k, vegf, her2, io, efflux, rss}
step_3_dot_product.what_it_measures: The raw alignment between patient biology and trial mechanism. High dot product = patient's active pathways match the trial's targeted pathways.

step_4_normalization.description: Divide by ‖t‖₂ — the L2 norm of the trial vector
step_4_normalization.why_normalize_by_trial_not_patient: Normalizing by ‖t‖₂ (not ‖p‖₂) means the score measures how well the patient matches the trial's requirements — not how active the patient's pathways are in absolute terms. A patient with very active pathways but misaligned with the trial still scores low. This is the key distinction from PATH B (cosine similarity), which normalizes by both vectors and loses this directionality.

step_5_clip.description: Clip to [0, 1] — scores cannot be negative or exceed 1
step_5_clip.ceiling: The theoretical ceiling is 1.0 when p and t are perfectly aligned. The practical ceiling for PATH A is 0.8898 (‖p_ref‖₂ constraint from reference patient vector).

step_6_output_labels.0.0_to_0.4: LOW — high failure risk; patient biology substantially misaligned with trial mechanism
step_6_output_labels.0.4_to_0.6: MODERATE — proceed with caution; partial alignment
step_6_output_labels.0.6_to_0.8: MODERATE-HIGH — favorable alignment; design gaps may still exist
step_6_output_labels.0.8_to_1.0: HIGH — strong fit; patient biology well-matched to trial mechanism

validated_runs.note: Only receipted two-layer demonstration. Responder score 0.8936 vs non-responder 0.6295. Delta value QUARANTINED (CT-03 — version conflict unresolved). Do not cite delta.

CAP_04_target_lock.name: Target-Lock Scoring Engine (Layer 1)

eight_metastasis_steps.step_1: Local invasion — tumor cells breach basement membrane and invade surrounding stroma
eight_metastasis_steps.step_2: Intravasation — tumor cells enter blood or lymphatic vessels
eight_metastasis_steps.step_3: Survival in circulation — tumor cells resist anoikis and immune surveillance in bloodstream
eight_metastasis_steps.step_4: Arrest at distant site — tumor cells adhere to endothelium at metastatic site
eight_metastasis_steps.step_5: Extravasation — tumor cells exit vessels at distant site
eight_metastasis_steps.step_6: Micrometastasis formation — tumor cells survive and proliferate in foreign microenvironment
eight_metastasis_steps.step_7: Colonization — micrometastases establish vascular supply and immune evasion

step_1_evo2_7b.what_it_scores: Evolutionary constraint — how conserved is this gene across species? Highly conserved genes are more likely to be essential drivers. Evo2-7B computes the likelihood of the wild-type sequence vs. mutant sequence under evolutionary selection pressure.
step_1_evo2_7b.output: Evolutionary likelihood score [0, 1] — higher = more evolutionarily constrained = more likely to be a genuine driver

step_2_enformer.what_it_scores: Chromatin accessibility and gene regulatory activity — is this gene actively transcribed in the relevant cancer cell type? Enformer predicts ATAC-seq and ChIP-seq profiles from DNA sequence.
step_2_enformer.output: Regulatory activity score [0, 1] — higher = more actively regulated in cancer context

step_3_alphafold3.what_it_scores: Structural confidence — does this gene encode a protein with a well-defined 3D structure that can be targeted by a drug? Disordered proteins are harder to drug.
step_3_alphafold3.output: pLDDT structural confidence score [0, 100] — higher = more structured = more druggable

step_4_composite.description: Composite Target-Lock score from three model outputs
step_4_composite.rationale: Threshold set to maximize sensitivity for known FDA-approved targets while excluding false positives from the 38-gene panel

validated_performance.retroactive_validation: 9 FDA-approved genes validated retroactively in predictions_2024_retroactive.json

CAP_04_target_lock.ceacam5_status: NOT IN 38-GENE PANEL — Target-Lock run has not been executed for CEACAM5. Any claim that CEACAM5 scores HIGH is EXPECTED (analyst inference), not COMPUTED. Must be labeled as prospective.

program_CEACAM5.glb_set_investment: Not quantified in source files — 5 trials

what_ceacam5_is.biology: CEACAM5 is a GPI-anchored glycoprotein overexpressed on the surface of colorectal, lung, gastric, and pancreatic cancer cells. It mediates cell-cell adhesion, promotes invasion and metastasis, and suppresses immune recognition by binding inhibitory receptors on NK cells and T cells. In normal tissue, CEACAM5 expression is restricted to the apical surface of intestinal epithelium — making it a tumor-selective target with a therapeutic window.
what_ceacam5_is.why_it_matters_in_crc: CEACAM5 is expressed in 98.7% of CRC tumors (Jansen et al. Cancers 2024). MSS CRC has HIGHER CEACAM5 expression than MSI-H CRC (MMR deficiency reduces CEA production, P<0.0001). This makes CEACAM5 one of the most tumor-selective targets in MSS mCRC — the challenge is not target expression but patient selection threshold.
what_ceacam5_is.serum_cea_as_proxy: Serum CEA (cCEA) is a liquid biopsy proxy for CEACAM5 IHC expression. cCEA ≥100 ng/mL correlates with IHC high-expressing tumors (Spearman ρ=0.43, n=92). In NSCLC: cCEA ≥100 ORR 41.7% vs 8.1% (P=0.003) in CEACAM5-high patients.

two_gate_architecture.description: CrisPRO's two-gate patient selection framework for CEACAM5-targeted programs

gate_1.name: CEACAM5 Expression Gate
gate_1.threshold: IHC ≥80% (H-score or percentage of cells staining ≥2+)
gate_1.rationale: CARMEN-LC03 enrolled at IHC ≥50% — this captured 72% of patients who could not respond. The ≥80% subgroup showed directional OS benefit (HR=0.71, post-hoc). The ≥50–79% subgroup showed numerically harmful PFS (HR=1.38). The gate must be ≥80% to enrich for responders.
gate_1.population_fraction: ~28% of MSS mCRC patients (OPEN_ASSUMPTION OA-08 — CARMEN-LC03 NSCLC proxy; CRC-specific data not available)
gate_1.liquid_biopsy_proxy: cCEA ≥100 ng/mL (NSCLC data only — CRC application by inference)
gate_1.crc_caveat: In PROCEADE-CRC-01 (N=40), IHC Spearman rho=−0.14 (P=0.4) — IHC is NOT predictive in CRC at any threshold tested. The second gate is mandatory for CRC programs.

gate_2.name: IO Permissiveness Gate
gate_2.threshold: pTMB ≥28 OR Immunoscore-IC high OR CD8+ TIL density high
gate_2.rationale: CEACAM5-targeted ADCs kill tumor cells but do not activate the immune system. In MSS CRC, the tumor microenvironment is immunosuppressive — killed cells are not immunogenic without an inflamed TME. Gate 2 selects patients whose TME can amplify the ADC's cytotoxic effect through immune activation.
gate_2.why_gate_2_is_mandatory_in_crc: PROCEADE-CRC-01 showed IHC is not predictive in CRC. The responder subgroup (3/40 = 7.5% ORR) likely had both high CEACAM5 expression AND an inflamed TME. Without Gate 2, the signal is diluted by immunosuppressed patients.

vector_scalpel_ranking.description: 11-dimension biomarker ranking for CEACAM5 patient selection — which biomarker best identifies the responder subpopulation

ranking.rationale: Liquid biopsy — no biopsy required, real-time monitoring, correlates with IHC
Strongest IO enrichment signal in MSS CRC from randomized data (CO.26 OS HR=0.34)
Tissue-based — requires biopsy, archival tissue may not reflect current expression
CD8+ TIL density at tumor center and invasive margin

trials.primary_result: PFS HR 1.14 (95% CI 0.86–1.51; P=0.8204) — FAILED primary endpoint

D1_biology.rationale: CEACAM5 is a genuine surface target — biology is sound. Low D1 score because the target is real.

D2_selection.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. IHC ≥50% threshold enrolled 72% of patients who could not respond. The ≥50–79% subgroup showed numerically harmful PFS (HR=1.38). The responder subgroup (IHC ≥80%) was diluted by non-responders. This is the canonical D2 Selection failure.

D3_architecture.rationale: Phase III design was appropriate — randomized, powered, pre-specified endpoint.

D4_timing.rationale: 2L+ timing is appropriate for this mechanism.

D6_resistance.rationale: DM4 payload resistance possible but not the primary driver.

D8_systemic.rationale: No safety hold or regulatory issue.

trials.responder_result: OS HR=0.71 (post-hoc, directional — CI not published; underpowered)
trials.transfer_lesson: Set IHC ≥80% as the enrollment gate. Add IO permissiveness as Gate 2. Consider cCEA ≥100 as liquid biopsy screening tool.

D1_biology.rationale: CEACAM5 is expressed in 98.7% of CRC — biology is sound.

D2_selection.rationale: IHC Spearman rho=−0.14 (P=0.4) — IHC is NOT predictive in CRC at any threshold. The 3 responders likely had both high CEACAM5 AND inflamed TME. Without Gate 2 (IO permissiveness), the signal is diluted.

D3_architecture.rationale: Phase II single-arm — appropriate for signal-finding.

D6_resistance.rationale: MSS CRC TME is immunosuppressive — DM4 payload may not trigger immunogenic cell death in this context.

D7_translational.rationale: NSCLC data does not directly translate to CRC — different TME biology.

trials.key_finding: IHC is not predictive in CRC — Gate 2 (IO permissiveness) is mandatory
trials.transfer_lesson: For CRC CEACAM5 programs: IHC alone is insufficient. Must add IO permissiveness gate (pTMB ≥28 or Immunoscore-IC high).

trials.primary_result: ORR 1.2% (1/86 confirmed PR); mPFS 3.6 months; mOS 6.9 months

D2_selection.rationale: Unselected population — no IHC threshold, no IO gate.

D6_resistance.rationale: SN-38 payload — topoisomerase I inhibitor. Resistance mechanisms well-characterized. Govitecan (SN-38) is less potent than Topo1i payloads in newer ADCs.

D7_translational.rationale: Older ADC technology — linker stability and bystander effect inferior to newer platforms.

trials.transfer_lesson: Payload matters as much as target. SN-38 is inferior to DM4 and Topo1i for CEACAM5 ADCs. Next-generation programs (SAR445953 — Topo1i payload) address this.

trials.drug: Cibisatamab (CEA-TCB) + FAP-4-1BBL — bispecific T-cell engager + costimulatory

D1_biology.rationale: CEA-TCB mechanism is biologically sound — redirects T cells to CEACAM5+ tumor cells.

D2_selection.rationale: Phase I — no selection gate applied. 13.5% ORR in unselected population suggests a responder subgroup exists.

D8_systemic.rationale: CRS 57.7% — significant safety signal. Grade ≥3 CRS 3.8% is manageable but requires careful monitoring.

trials.transfer_lesson: 13.5% ORR in unselected mCRC is the highest CEACAM5 ORR in CRC to date. With Gate 2 (IO permissiveness), the responder ORR could be substantially higher. CRS management is the key safety challenge.

trials.primary_result: EU clinical hold — no efficacy data published

D8_systemic.rationale: EU clinical hold — regulatory/safety issue. No efficacy data available.

trials.transfer_lesson: Next-generation Topo1i payload (SAR445953) is the successor program. SGN-CEACAM5C EU hold does not invalidate the CEACAM5 target — it validates the need for better payload selection.

sanofi_engagement_specifics.pitch_anchor: CARMEN-LC03 D2 failure decode → two-gate solution → SAR445953 patient selection package
sanofi_engagement_specifics.key_claim: CrisPRO decoded CARMEN-LC03's failure and identified the IHC ≥80% + IO permissiveness gate. For SAR445953: CrisPRO can produce the patient selection package before the trial reads out.

what_crispro_proved: CARMEN-LC03 (Phase III, N=389): PFS HR 1.14 (P=0.82) at IHC ≥50% — D2 Selection failure decoded
CEACAM5 IHC ≥80% OS HR=0.71 (post-hoc, directional — CI not published; underpowered)
CEACAM5 50–79% PFS HR=1.38 (numerically harmful — confirms ≥50% threshold is wrong)
cCEA ≥100 ORR 41.7% vs 8.1% (P=0.003) in CEACAM5-high NSCLC — liquid biopsy proxy validated
IHC ↔ cCEA Spearman ρ=0.43 (n=92) — moderate correlation
PROCEADE-CRC-01 (N=40): ORR 7.5%; IHC Spearman rho=−0.14 (P=0.4) — IHC not predictive in CRC
CEACAM5 any-positivity in CRC: 98.7% (Jansen et al. Cancers 2024)
MSS CRC has HIGHER CEACAM5 expression than MSI-H CRC (MMR deficiency reduces CEA, P<0.0001)
program_CEACAM5.what_crispro_can_offer: For any CEACAM5-targeted program: CrisPRO decoded CARMEN-LC03's D2 Selection failure and identified the two-gate framework (IHC ≥80% + IO permissiveness). For CRC programs: IHC alone is insufficient — Gate 2 is mandatory. CrisPRO can produce an ENRICH/CONSIDER/EXCLUDE stratification table, Vector-Scalpel biomarker ranking, and protocol enrollment language for any CEACAM5 program.
caveats: CEACAM5 ≥80% OS HR=0.71 is post-hoc, underpowered, CI not published — label as directional trend
cCEA ≥100 data is from NSCLC only — CRC application is by inference
CEACAM5 ≥80% prevalence in CRC (~28%) is OPEN_ASSUMPTION OA-08 — CARMEN-LC03 NSCLC proxy
CEACAM5 Target-Lock run has not been executed — EXPECTED, not COMPUTED

what_ddr_is.pathway: DNA Damage Response (DDR) — the cellular machinery that detects and repairs DNA damage

key_kinases.ATR: ATR (Ataxia Telangiectasia and Rad3-related) — activated by single-stranded DNA breaks and replication stress. Phosphorylates CHK1 to halt replication and allow repair.
key_kinases.WEE1: WEE1 — phosphorylates CDK1/CDK2 to prevent premature mitotic entry when DNA is damaged. WEE1 inhibition forces cells with unrepaired DNA into mitosis → mitotic catastrophe.
key_kinases.PARP: PARP1/2 — repairs single-strand breaks via base excision repair. PARP inhibition in BRCA-mutant cells causes synthetic lethality (double-strand breaks cannot be repaired by HRR).

what_ddr_is.replication_stress: Replication stress (RS) occurs when replication forks stall or collapse — common in cancer cells with oncogene activation (KRAS, MYC, CCNE1). RS-High tumors are dependent on ATR for survival — ATR inhibition kills them selectively.
what_ddr_is.why_ddr_fails_in_unselected_populations: DDR inhibitors are highly effective in the RS-High or HRR-deficient subpopulation but have no activity in RS-Low or HRR-proficient patients. Enrolling unselected populations dilutes the signal to noise — the responder ORR (40%) is hidden inside an overall ORR (17%) that looks marginal.

rs_score_algorithm.description: Replication Stress (RS) score — classifies patients as RS-High or RS-Low
rs_score_algorithm.what_it_measures: The degree of replication fork stalling and DNA damage in the tumor. RS-High tumors are dependent on ATR for survival.
rs_score_algorithm.clinical_validation: Berzosertib: RS-High ORR 40% vs RS-Low ORR 5% — 8× difference. This is the strongest biomarker-stratified signal in the ATR/DDR corpus.

trials.primary_result: ORR 17% overall; RS-High subgroup ORR 40% vs RS-Low ORR 5%

D1_biology.rationale: ATR is a genuine target in RS-High tumors — biology is sound.

D2_selection.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. RS-High gate was not applied at enrollment. RS-High ORR 40% vs RS-Low ORR 5% — 8× difference. The responder subgroup was diluted by RS-Low patients who cannot respond to ATR inhibition.

D3_architecture.rationale: Phase I/II design appropriate for signal-finding.

D6_resistance.rationale: RS-Low patients have intrinsic resistance — not acquired resistance.

trials.transfer_lesson: Apply RS-High gate at enrollment. RS score must be computed from tumor biopsy before randomization.

D1_biology.rationale: WEE1 inhibition is biologically sound in CDK2-active tumors.

D2_selection.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. PTEN-loss creates CDK2 dependency that paradoxically PROTECTS against WEE1 inhibition — PTEN-loss cells have alternative CDK2 regulation. PTEN-intact cells are dependent on WEE1 for G2/M checkpoint. Enrolling PTEN-loss patients is not just unhelpful — it is actively harmful (PFS HR 1.82).

D6_resistance.rationale: PTEN-loss is a resistance mechanism — not acquired but intrinsic. PTEN-loss cells bypass WEE1 inhibition via PI3K/AKT-mediated CDK2 regulation.

trials.transfer_lesson: PTEN status must be assessed before enrollment. PTEN-loss is a contraindication for WEE1 inhibition. This is the canonical example of a biomarker that is both predictive of benefit AND predictive of harm.

D1_biology.rationale: ATR+PARP combination is biologically sound — ATR inhibition creates replication stress that PARP cannot repair.

D2_selection.rationale: PRIMARY FAILURE DOMAIN. Post-PARPi patients have acquired resistance mechanisms that bypass both ATR and PARP inhibition. PARPi-naive patients have intact HRR machinery that is sensitized by ATR inhibition. Enrolling post-PARPi patients dilutes the signal from PARPi-naive patients (ORR 36% vs 4%).

D6_resistance.rationale: Post-PARPi resistance is the dominant failure mechanism — BRCA reversion mutations, ABCB1 upregulation, and RAD51 restoration all bypass the ATR+PARP combination.

trials.transfer_lesson: PARPi-naive status must be a mandatory enrollment criterion for ATR+PARP combinations. Post-PARPi patients should be excluded or enrolled in a separate arm with a different combination strategy.

trials.primary_result: STK11/KEAP1-loss subgroup showed benefit; STK11-intact did not. Preliminary data.

latify_two_layer_demonstration.description: LATIFY is the ONLY receipted two-layer demonstration of CrisPRO's mechanism fit engine
latify_two_layer_demonstration.layer_1_result: Target-Lock: ceralasertib targets ATR — confirmed genuine driver in STK11/KEAP1-mutant NSCLC (replication stress elevated)
latify_two_layer_demonstration.layer_2_result: Mechanism Fit: responder score 0.8936 vs non-responder score 0.6295
latify_two_layer_demonstration.delta_status: QUARANTINED — CT-03 blocker: patent delta +0.366 vs receipt delta +0.2641 — version conflict unresolved. Do not cite any specific delta value.
latify_two_layer_demonstration.what_to_cite: Responder score 0.8936 vs non-responder 0.6295 — this is the correct citation

D2_selection.rationale: STK11/KEAP1 gate identifies the responder subpopulation. STK11-intact patients do not respond — the gate is necessary.

D5_combination.rationale: ATRi + IO combination is mechanistically sound — ATR inhibition increases neoantigen presentation and PD-L1 expression, sensitizing tumors to checkpoint blockade.

trials.transfer_lesson: STK11/KEAP1 mutation status must be assessed before enrollment. The two-layer demonstration shows CrisPRO can identify the responder subpopulation before the trial reads out.

D3_architecture.rationale: Phase I dose-finding — not designed to detect efficacy signal.

trials.transfer_lesson: Dose-finding data establishes the safety profile for Phase II. The CAPRI Phase II is the efficacy read-out.

what_crispro_proved: Berzosertib: RS-High ORR 40% vs RS-Low ORR 5% — 8× difference; D2 Selection failure decoded
Adavosertib: PTEN-intact ORR 23% vs PTEN-loss ORR 0%; PFS HR 0.55 vs 1.82 — PTEN-loss is HARMFUL
CAPRI: PARPi-naive ORR 36% vs post-PARPi ORR 4%; PFS HR 0.42 vs 1.31 — post-PARPi is HARMFUL
GLB set: 4 ATR/DDR trials representing $1.95B in failed program investment decoded
LATIFY: responder score 0.8936 vs non-responder 0.6295 — only receipted two-layer demonstration (delta QUARANTINED)
program_ATR_DDR.what_crispro_can_offer: For any ATR/DDR abstract: identify which biomarker gate is missing (RS-High, PTEN-intact, PARPi-naive) and what the responder ORR would be with correct patient selection. For new DDR programs: apply the 8D framework to predict the failure mode before enrollment. CrisPRO has decoded $1.95B in failed ATR/DDR investment.
caveats: LATIFY delta is QUARANTINED — do not cite a specific delta value
GLB set IP valuation figures are benchmarks, not guarantees
RS score algorithm is not publicly available — CrisPRO uses a proprietary implementation
